PROOF OF SERVICE OF MOTION FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.
REPLY TO OPPOS TO ITS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO STANTEC'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANT STANTEC IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE FACTS WHICH SHOW CROSS-DEFENDANT TURNER OWES CROSS-COMPLAINANT A DUTY. WITHOUT CONTRACTUAL PRIVITY, IT APPEARS THAT A SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN TURNER AND STANTEC MUST BE PLEADED. J'AIRE CORP. V. GREGORY, 24 CAL.3D 799 (1979). GENERALLY THAT SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP IS NOT FOUND AS BETWEEN CONSTRUCTION MANAGERS AND ARCHITECTS. RATCLIFF ARCHITECTS V. VANIR CONTRUCTION MANGEMENT, INC., 88 CAL.APP.4TH 595 (2001). THE PRESENT COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT TURNER OWNED A DUTY TO STANTEC BUT DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS TO ESTABLISH THAT DUTY, SUCH AS THE BIAKANJA FACTORS DISCUSSED IN J'AIRE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Stantec'S X-Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID V. ROSE OF CANEVENTER BLACK LLP, TEL. NO. (626) 768-1285 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
ATTY. MARK J. D. ARGENIO OF WOOD SMITH HENNING & BERMAN LLP, TEL. NO. (925)356-8205 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Stantec's CROSS-Complaint is Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Cross-Complainant Stantec is granted leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege facts which show Cross-Defendant Turner owes Cross-Complainant a duty. Without contractual privity, it appears that a special relationship between Turner and Stantec must be pleaded. J'Aire Corp. v. Gregory, 24 Cal.3d 799 (1979). Generally that special relationship is not found as between construction managers and architects. Ratcliff Architects v. Vanir Contruction Mangement, Inc., 88 Cal.App.4th 595 (2001). The present complaint alleges that Turner owned a duty to Stantec but does not plead facts to establish that duty, such as the Biakanja factors discussed in J'Aire. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Curtis Karnow; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =(302/CK)
